steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Points drop $25\%$ every $4$ seconds, starting at $640$. A $25\%$ decrease means each interval keeps $75\%$, so each $4$-second interval multiplies the score by $0.75$. Since $x$ seconds contains $\frac{x}{4}$ of those intervals, the model is $$640(0.75)^{x/4}.$$

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "$f(x)=640(0.75)^{\frac{x}{4}}$": decay factor 0.75 per 4-second interval, exponent $x/4$ counts intervals. ✓
  • "$f(x)=640(0.75)^{4x}$": exponent $4x$ multiplies intervals by 4 instead of dividing; way too fast a decay. ✗
  • "$f(x)=640(0.25)^{\frac{x}{4}}$": 0.25 is the amount lost, not the amount kept; this drops to nearly zero far too fast. ✗
  • "$f(x)=640(0.25)^{4x}$": wrong factor and wrong exponent. ✗
That matches the correct answer; A is correct.
